---
title: "External validation — BC drill-hole distribution blind test"
subtitle: "BC Golden Triangle"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
---

The BCGT counterpart of the EastAK [`validation.qmd`](../eastak/validation.qmd).
BCGT is where the portfolio runs its distribution-based external
blind test. BCGS GeoFile 2025-11 (Feb 2025) publishes 366 post-2015
drill collars in our AOI with per-hole element maxima. That lets us
build distributions of model P at drilled positives vs drilled
negatives, in cells that were vs weren't already in our training
labels.

This is the strongest external validation the repo has. Everything else
in the v2 report rolls up from the numbers here.

```{python}
#| label: setup
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
from scipy.spatial import cKDTree

from ai_minerals.data._common import DATA_DERIVED, DATA_RAW
from ai_minerals.data.adapters import get_adapter
from ai_minerals.features.assemble import build_feature_frame
from ai_minerals.model import (
    non_feature_columns, add_lithology_onehot, build_training_set,
    sample_pseudo_negatives, success_rate_curve,
)
from ai_minerals.model_rf import count_feature_columns, make_rf
from ai_minerals.regions.bcgt import BCGT

df = pd.read_parquet(DATA_DERIVED / "features_bcgt_500m.parquet")
label_cols = tuple(f"is_{k}" for k in BCGT.deposit_classes)
df["is_any_deposit"] = (df[list(label_cols)].sum(axis=1) > 0).astype(np.uint8)
top_classes = df["lithology_class"].value_counts().head(10).index.tolist()
print(f"feature frame: {df.shape}  |  positives: {int(df['is_any_deposit'].sum())}")
```

## 1. Baseline RF (no count / has_data)

Same configuration as the [RF+SHAP notebook](random_forest_and_shap.qmd),
used here to score every cell in the AOI.

```{python}
#| label: fit-rf
X, y = build_training_set(
    df, top_classes, n_per_positive=30, random_state=42,
    label_col="is_any_deposit",
    label_cols=label_cols + ("is_any_deposit",),
)
drop_cols = count_feature_columns(list(X.columns))
X_trim = X.drop(columns=drop_cols)
feat_cols = list(X_trim.columns)

rf = make_rf()
rf.fit(X_trim.fillna(-9999).to_numpy(), y)

all_rows = add_lithology_onehot(df, top_classes)
non_feat = non_feature_columns(label_cols + ("is_any_deposit",))
X_all = all_rows.drop(columns=[c for c in all_rows.columns if c in non_feat] + ["lithology_class"])
X_all_trim = X_all.drop(columns=drop_cols)
p_fam = rf.predict_proba(X_all_trim.fillna(-9999).to_numpy())[:, 1]

print(f"X shape: {X_trim.shape}  features: {len(feat_cols)}")
pos_mask = df["is_any_deposit"].to_numpy() == 1
print(f"P at positives: median={np.median(p_fam[pos_mask]):.3f}  q25={np.quantile(p_fam[pos_mask], .25):.3f}")
print(f"P overall:      median={np.median(p_fam):.3f}     q95={np.quantile(p_fam, .95):.3f}")
```

## 2. Load the 366 post-2015 drill holes

```{python}
#| label: drillholes
dh = get_adapter("drillholes", "bcgs_geofile")(
    DATA_RAW / "bcgs_drillholes/bcgs_drillholes_bcgt.gpkg", BCGT.aoi
).to_crs(BCGT.working_crs)
print(f"post-2015 drill holes in AOI: {len(dh)}")
print(f"intersected (Cu≥0.2% | Mo≥0.03% | Au≥0.5g/t | Ag≥10g/t): {int(dh['intersected'].sum())}")
print(f"non-intersected: {int((~dh['intersected']).sum())}")
```

## 3. Classify each hole by training-label overlap

For each drill cell, decide whether the training labels know anything:

- **In-training:** cell already flagged as one of the 4 deposit classes
- **Near-occurrence:** cell has `any_mineral_occurrence=1` but no deposit-class label
- **Unknown to training:** cell has zero flags

```{python}
#| label: classify-holes
tree = cKDTree(df[["x","y"]].to_numpy())
dists, idx = tree.query(np.c_[dh.geometry.x, dh.geometry.y], k=1)

pos_labels = df.iloc[idx][list(label_cols)].to_numpy()
known_occ  = df.iloc[idx]["any_mineral_occurrence"].to_numpy()
in_training = pos_labels.any(axis=1)
near_known  = (~in_training) & (known_occ == 1)
unknown     = (~in_training) & (known_occ == 0)

hole_p = p_fam[idx]
dh_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "hole_id": dh["hole_id"].values,
    "intersected": dh["intersected"].values,
    "P": hole_p,
    "in_training_label": in_training,
    "near_known_occurrence": near_known,
    "unknown_to_training": unknown,
    "percentile": 100 * (p_fam[None, :] < hole_p[:, None]).mean(axis=1),
})

print(f"{'bucket':<42} {'intersected':>12} {'non-int':>10}")
for label, mask in [
    ("in cell with MINFILE deposit label",       in_training),
    ("near known occurrence, no deposit label",  near_known),
    ("TRAINING SET KNOWS NOTHING ABOUT CELL",    unknown),
]:
    i = int((dh_df["intersected"] & mask).sum())
    n = int((~dh_df["intersected"] & mask).sum())
    print(f"{label:<42} {i:>12} {n:>10}")
```

The bottom-right bucket is the cleanest blind test: drilled cells the
model has never seen a label for, split by whether they hit ore or not.

## 4. Blind-test distributions

```{python}
#| label: blind-dists
blind = dh_df[dh_df["unknown_to_training"]]
p_pos = blind[blind["intersected"]]["P"].values
p_neg = blind[~blind["intersected"]]["P"].values

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
bins_p = np.linspace(0, 1, 31)
axes[0].hist(p_neg, bins=bins_p, alpha=0.7, color="gray",
             label=f"blind negatives (N={len(p_neg)})")
axes[0].hist(p_pos, bins=bins_p, alpha=0.7, color="red",
             label=f"blind positives (N={len(p_pos)})")
axes[0].axvline(np.median(p_fam), ls="--", c="black",
                label=f"AOI median P={np.median(p_fam):.3f}")
axes[0].set_xlabel("P(any deposit)"); axes[0].set_ylabel("count")
axes[0].set_title("Blind positive vs negative — P distribution")
axes[0].legend(); axes[0].grid(alpha=0.3)

bins_pct = np.linspace(0, 100, 31)
axes[1].hist(blind[~blind["intersected"]]["percentile"], bins=bins_pct,
             alpha=0.7, color="gray", label=f"blind negatives (N={len(p_neg)})")
axes[1].hist(blind[blind["intersected"]]["percentile"], bins=bins_pct,
             alpha=0.7, color="red", label=f"blind positives (N={len(p_pos)})")
axes[1].set_xlabel("percentile within AOI"); axes[1].set_ylabel("count")
axes[1].set_title("Percentile distribution")
axes[1].legend(); axes[1].grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
```

## 5. Statistical tests

```{python}
#| label: stats
from scipy.stats import ks_2samp, mannwhitneyu
ks_stat, ks_p = ks_2samp(p_pos, p_neg)
mw_stat, mw_p = mannwhitneyu(p_pos, p_neg, alternative="greater")

print(f"=== Blind-test distributions, TRUE-BLIND bucket ({len(p_pos)} pos + {len(p_neg)} neg) ===")
print(f"  positives: median P={np.median(p_pos):.3f}  mean P={np.mean(p_pos):.3f}")
print(f"  negatives: median P={np.median(p_neg):.3f}  mean P={np.mean(p_neg):.3f}")
print(f"  K-S test (distributions differ):              D={ks_stat:.3f}  p={ks_p:.2e}")
print(f"  Mann-Whitney U (positives > negatives):       p={mw_p:.2e}")
```

## 6. Percentile-threshold capture

What fraction of the blind positives are flagged at each AOI-threshold?
And the concerning companion number: what fraction of the blind
negatives are flagged at the same threshold?

```{python}
#| label: threshold-capture
print(f"{'threshold':<14} {'blind pos flagged':>20} {'blind neg flagged':>20} {'lift':>8}")
for pct_thresh in [80, 90, 95, 99]:
    t = np.percentile(p_fam, pct_thresh)
    cap_pos = (p_pos >= t).mean()
    cap_neg = (p_neg >= t).mean()
    lift = cap_pos / max(cap_neg, 1e-6)
    print(f"  top {100-pct_thresh:>2}% cells   {100*cap_pos:>15.0f}%   {100*cap_neg:>15.0f}%   {lift:>7.2f}x")
```

**Interpretation.** A well-behaved model should have positives flagged
at much higher rates than negatives at every threshold. The BCGT result
shows the lift shrinking (or reversing) at strict cutoffs — reflecting
the v1 exploration-bias diagnosis applied at distribution scale.

## 7. In-distribution sanity check

Holes in cells we already labeled as positive — these are *not* a blind
test, but a sanity check: if the model doesn't rank its own training
positives well, we have a bug.

```{python}
#| label: in-training
in_tr = dh_df[dh_df["in_training_label"]]
print(f"In-training holes: {len(in_tr)}")
print(f"  intersected:     {int(in_tr['intersected'].sum())} ({100*in_tr['intersected'].mean():.0f}%)")
print(f"  median P:        {in_tr['P'].median():.3f}")
print(f"  median percentile: {in_tr['percentile'].median():.1f}%")
```

Expected: in-training holes should have P near 1 and percentile near
100 — the model trained on these cells as positives and should rank
them high. If not, something is broken in the feature pipeline or the
label-assignment step.

## Summary

- **241 truly-blind drilled cells** (154 positives + 87 negatives, all
  in cells the training labels have zero info about).
- Statistical test for "positives > negatives" on these blind cells:
  K-S says the distributions differ; Mann-Whitney says the direction
  isn't what we'd want.
- At strict cutoffs (top 5 %) the model flags blind negatives at
  roughly the same rate as blind positives — the drilled-nothing cells
  cluster in already-explored terrain near training positives, so they
  look as "prospective" to the model as cells that actually host ore.
- The exploration-bias framing identified in the EastAK sensitivity
  pass (three channels by which training-label signal correlates with
  exploration history) plays out at distribution scale here: blind
  negatives — drilled cells with no economic result — score just as
  high as blind positives because both populations sit in already-
  explored terrain.

**What this does NOT prove.** That the model would find *unknown*
mineralization in unexplored terrain. The blind-test distributions tell
us how much lift the model gives over random at each cutoff; they don't
tell us whether the model's top-ranked previously-unknown cells are
where real new discoveries would come from. That's the multi-year
experiment no portfolio demo can run.

## 8. Drill-as-feature sensitivity — does feeding drill data back into the model help?

Natural follow-up: if the 366 post-2015 drill collars carry actual
ground-truth assays, what if we feed half of them back to the model as
*input features* (per-cell aggregates of nearby intersected holes), and
hold the other half out as the blind test? Two competing hypotheses:

1. **Naïve hopeful:** drill-derived features add real assayed-grade
   signal that geochem aggregates can't match (only ~366 drilled cells,
   but they have core-grade Cu/Au/Mo/Ag values, not stream-sediment
   proxies). Blind-positive capture rises.
2. **Bias-aware skeptic:** drill cells are densely concentrated in the
   same already-explored terrain that the MINFILE positives cluster in.
   Adding them as features rides the *same exploration-bias axis* the
   pseudo-supervised pipeline already has, so the new features mostly
   replicate signal the geochem features already provide — and blind-
   negative cells continue to score as high as blind-positives because
   they're in the same terrain.

The experiment: random 50/50 split of the 366 holes by `hole_id`. The
training-side holes feed into six per-cell features
(`n_drill_5km`, `n_intersected_5km`, `max_cu_drill_5km`,
`max_mo_drill_5km`, `max_au_drill_5km`, `max_ag_drill_5km`). Train
two RFs on identical MINFILE positives + pseudo-negatives — one with
those features, one without. Score the held-out half of drill collars
on each model.

```{python}
#| label: drill-split-experiment
from scipy.stats import ks_2samp, mannwhitneyu

rng = np.random.default_rng(seed=42)
mask_train_half = rng.random(len(dh)) < 0.5
dh_train = dh[mask_train_half].reset_index(drop=True)
dh_held  = dh[~mask_train_half].reset_index(drop=True)

# Build training-half drill-derived features over all AOI cells
train_xy = np.c_[dh_train.geometry.x, dh_train.geometry.y]
tree_train = cKDTree(train_xy)
cell_xy = df[["x", "y"]].to_numpy()
neighbor_lists = tree_train.query_ball_point(cell_xy, r=5000.0)

for c in ["max_cu_pct", "max_mo_pct", "max_au_gpt", "max_ag_gpt"]:
    dh_train[c] = pd.to_numeric(dh_train[c], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
intersected_arr = dh_train["intersected"].astype(int).to_numpy()
cu_arr = dh_train["max_cu_pct"].to_numpy()
mo_arr = dh_train["max_mo_pct"].to_numpy()
au_arr = dh_train["max_au_gpt"].to_numpy()
ag_arr = dh_train["max_ag_gpt"].astype(float).fillna(0).to_numpy()

n_drill = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.int32)
n_int   = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.int32)
max_cu  = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.float32)
max_mo  = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.float32)
max_au  = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.float32)
max_ag  = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=np.float32)
for i, idxs in enumerate(neighbor_lists):
    if not idxs: continue
    n_drill[i] = len(idxs)
    n_int[i]   = int(intersected_arr[idxs].sum())
    max_cu[i] = float(cu_arr[idxs].max())
    max_mo[i] = float(mo_arr[idxs].max())
    max_au[i] = float(au_arr[idxs].max())
    max_ag[i] = float(ag_arr[idxs].max())

df_aug = df.copy()
df_aug["n_drill_5km"]       = n_drill
df_aug["n_intersected_5km"] = n_int
df_aug["max_cu_drill_5km"]  = max_cu
df_aug["max_mo_drill_5km"]  = max_mo
df_aug["max_au_drill_5km"]  = max_au
df_aug["max_ag_drill_5km"]  = max_ag

print(f"Cells with ≥1 nearby training-half drill: {(n_drill > 0).sum():,}/{len(df):,}")
print(f"Cells with ≥1 intersected nearby:         {(n_int   > 0).sum():,}/{len(df):,}")
```

```{python}
#| label: drill-split-train
from ai_minerals.model_rf import feature_importance
from ai_minerals.model import non_feature_columns

label_cols_full = label_cols + ("is_any_deposit",)

def train_and_score(label_df):
    X, y = build_training_set(
        label_df, top_classes, n_per_positive=30, random_state=42,
        label_col="is_any_deposit", label_cols=label_cols_full,
    )
    X = X.drop(columns=count_feature_columns(list(X.columns)))
    rf_ = make_rf(); rf_.fit(X.fillna(-9999).to_numpy(), y)
    all_rows = add_lithology_onehot(label_df, top_classes)
    nf = non_feature_columns(label_cols_full)
    X_all = all_rows.drop(columns=[c for c in all_rows.columns if c in nf] + ["lithology_class"])
    X_all = X_all.drop(columns=count_feature_columns(list(X_all.columns)))
    p_ = rf_.predict_proba(X_all.fillna(-9999).to_numpy())[:, 1]
    return rf_, p_, list(X.columns)

rf_base, p_base, feat_base = train_and_score(df)
rf_aug,  p_aug,  feat_aug  = train_and_score(df_aug)
print(f"baseline   features: {len(feat_base)}")
print(f"augmented  features: {len(feat_aug)}  (+6 drill-derived)")

NEW_FEATURES = ["n_drill_5km","n_intersected_5km","max_cu_drill_5km",
                "max_mo_drill_5km","max_au_drill_5km","max_ag_drill_5km"]
fi_aug = feature_importance(rf_aug, feat_aug).head(15)
print("\nTop-15 features in augmented model:")
print(fi_aug.to_string(index=False))
print(f"\nDrill-derived features in top-15: {fi_aug['feature'].isin(NEW_FEATURES).sum()}/{len(NEW_FEATURES)}")
```

```{python}
#| label: drill-split-score
tree_cells = cKDTree(df[["x","y"]].to_numpy())
held_xy = np.c_[dh_held.geometry.x, dh_held.geometry.y]
_, hole_idx = tree_cells.query(held_xy, k=1)
intersected_held = dh_held["intersected"].astype(bool).to_numpy()

# Stratify
pos_at_hole = df.iloc[hole_idx][list(label_cols)].to_numpy()
known_at_hole = df.iloc[hole_idx]["any_mineral_occurrence"].to_numpy()
in_training  = pos_at_hole.any(axis=1)
unknown      = (~in_training) & (known_at_hole == 0)

print(f"held-out drill collars: {len(dh_held)}")
print(f"  in_training (sanity)  : {int(in_training.sum())}")
print(f"  TRULY BLIND            : {int(unknown.sum())}  ({int((unknown & intersected_held).sum())} pos + {int((unknown & ~intersected_held).sum())} neg)")
print()

print(f"{'metric':<32} {'baseline':>14} {'augmented':>14}")
for label, pa in [("baseline", p_base), ("augmented", p_aug)]:
    pass
bp_base = p_base[hole_idx][unknown & intersected_held]
bn_base = p_base[hole_idx][unknown & ~intersected_held]
bp_aug  = p_aug [hole_idx][unknown & intersected_held]
bn_aug  = p_aug [hole_idx][unknown & ~intersected_held]

def line(label, vb, va):
    print(f"{label:<32} {vb:>14} {va:>14}")
line("blind-pos median P",       f"{np.median(bp_base):.3f}",          f"{np.median(bp_aug):.3f}")
line("blind-neg median P",       f"{np.median(bn_base):.3f}",          f"{np.median(bn_aug):.3f}")
line("MW-U (pos > neg) p",       f"{mannwhitneyu(bp_base, bn_base, alternative='greater').pvalue:.2e}",
                                  f"{mannwhitneyu(bp_aug,  bn_aug,  alternative='greater').pvalue:.2e}")
ks_b = ks_2samp(bp_base, bn_base); ks_a = ks_2samp(bp_aug, bn_aug)
line("K-S D / p",                f"{ks_b.statistic:.3f} / {ks_b.pvalue:.2e}",
                                  f"{ks_a.statistic:.3f} / {ks_a.pvalue:.2e}")

t90_b = np.percentile(p_base, 90); t90_a = np.percentile(p_aug, 90)
t95_b = np.percentile(p_base, 95); t95_a = np.percentile(p_aug, 95)
line("top-10% pos / neg flagged", f"{100*(bp_base>=t90_b).mean():.0f}% / {100*(bn_base>=t90_b).mean():.0f}%",
                                   f"{100*(bp_aug >=t90_a).mean():.0f}% / {100*(bn_aug >=t90_a).mean():.0f}%")
line("top-5%  pos / neg flagged", f"{100*(bp_base>=t95_b).mean():.0f}% / {100*(bn_base>=t95_b).mean():.0f}%",
                                   f"{100*(bp_aug >=t95_a).mean():.0f}% / {100*(bn_aug >=t95_a).mean():.0f}%")
```

**Result.** Drill-derived features barely move anything:

- **0 of 6 drill features land in the top-15 importance.** The
  classical pathfinder geochem (Bi/Pb/Ag/As `*_max_5km` and `*_mean_5km`)
  still dominates. The new features rank below 15 because the model
  already gets the same regional signal — at higher coverage — from
  AGDB4 stream-sediment + soil samples (~2,000 in AOI) versus only ~180
  training-half drill cells.
- **Blind-positive median P** rises slightly (0.882 → 0.914), so the
  augmented model gives positives a small bump.
- **Blind-negative median P** doesn't budge (0.975 → 0.976) — drilled-
  nothing cells sit in the same explored terrain regardless of whether
  the model has access to drill features.
- **Top-5 % threshold lift** is unchanged: 0.76× either way (negatives
  win at strict cutoffs in both models).
- **K-S statistic** is unchanged at 0.319.

This is the bias-aware-skeptic prediction in clean form. Drill-derived
per-cell features ride the same exploration-density axis the geochem
aggregates already do; the model can't tell apart "this cell has
intersected drill data nearby" from "this cell has lots of stream-
sediment samples nearby" because both proxies for "this terrain has
been worked."

**What WOULD potentially help** (out of scope here, but worth naming):

- **Per-hole assays as positives, not features.** If the 154 blind-
  positive drill collars are in cells the training labels don't know
  about, treating their assays as additional training labels rather
  than features is a different experiment — and it would inflate the
  training-positive count, which would help training-label metrics but
  wouldn't address the blind-negative-as-high-as-blind-positive problem.
- **Drill data as a *negative*-class refinement.** The 137 drilled-
  nothing cells are the cleanest non-deposit labels we have — much
  better than pseudo-negatives. Replacing the pseudo-negative pool with
  drilled-nothing cells (in the training half) would test whether the
  *negative-class definition* is what's driving the bias. Plausibly
  more useful than drill-as-feature.
- **Causal exploration-effort covariates.** Frame "has been drilled" as
  a treatment variable and the training-label as outcome; estimate a
  propensity score for each cell; reweight or stratify accordingly.
  This is the principled way to control for exploration bias in MPM
  papers from 2024-2025 (see v2.1 next-steps in the integrated report).

**What's next (v2.1 ideas).** See the [integrated report's "Next
steps"](bcgt_porphyry_prospectivity.qmd#next-steps-v12-ideas-un-built) section.
